meta.title: Sasha — iOS / SwiftUI Developer
meta.description: Sasha — iOS / SwiftUI developer building GloboSense, a video interpreting platform. Student of Wirtschaft & Informatik in Germany.

hero.title_line2: I build iOS apps with SwiftUI.
hero.description: I’m a developer and a Wirtschaft & Informatik student in Germany. Right now I’m working on GloboSense — a video interpreting app that helps people find human translators (including sign-language interpreters).

about.title: About me
about.text: I like building clean, practical products and iterating fast. I’ve worked hands-on across app UI, backend integration, and realtime calling features.
about.li1: Focus: iOS development, product MVPs, and scalability thinking
about.li2: Also enjoy: Unity prototyping, 3D modeling in Blender

building.title: What I’m building now
building.text: GloboSense — a platform for people who need a translator. The goal is to make it easy to schedule or instantly call an interpreter, and support interpreter agencies too.

skills.title: Skills snapshot
skills.li1: iOS: Swift, SwiftUI, UIKit basics, app architecture, performance debugging

hero.title: My Work Experience
hero.subtitle: A focused overview of projects, roles, and technical contributions I’ve made so far.

globosense.title: GloboSense — iOS Developer
globosense.li1: Architected SwiftUI interface and navigation structure.
globosense.li2: Integrated authentication, database logic, and realtime features.
globosense.li3: Worked with video call SDKs and debugged live media pipelines.
globosense.li4: Optimized state management and performance for scalability.

independent.title: Independent Development
independent.li1: Built multiple prototype apps to test UI systems and architectures.
independent.li2: Experimented with backend logic and deployment flows.
independent.li3: Studied performance, memory usage, and realtime networking behavior.

direction.title: Current Direction
direction.text: I’m currently focused on becoming a strong product-minded iOS engineer who understands both user experience and system architecture. I enjoy building fast, testing ideas quickly, and improving designs iteratively.

projects.title: My projects
projects.text: Public showcase projects I can share right now. For now, this list includes EchoMind.

meta.title: EchoMind — Voice Notes to Clear Actions
meta.description: EchoMind transforms voice recordings into transcriptions, summaries, and actionable notes.

brand.name: EchoMind
brand.tagline: Voice capture, structured notes, and follow-up clarity

hero.title: EchoMind
hero.subtitle: Record a thought. Get a clean note.
hero.intro: EchoMind is an iOS voice-first notes app in active development. It captures spoken thoughts and turns them into transcripts, summaries, and clearer next actions.
hero.status_note: This is not the final version. UI and feature depth are actively improving over time.

flow.title: Current prototype flow
flow.li1: Home screen with one-tap recording and quick entry points for ideas, study, and tasks.
flow.li2: Library with recordings, timestamps, and duration previews.
flow.li3: Recording details with playback, transcription, and summary blocks.
flow.li4: Translate actions for transcript and summary from the same screen.
flow.li5: Settings for model selection, language, sounds, haptics, storage, and export defaults.

ai.title: AI output today
ai.text: The current output in screenshots already shows structured interpretation beyond plain text conversion.
ai.li1: Detects likely reference types (for example plans/schedule, humor/phrase).
ai.li2: Highlights date and time cues found inside free speech.
ai.li3: Supports quick re-transcribe and re-summarize iterations.
ai.li4: Adds translation hooks to keep notes accessible across languages.

roadmap.title: What is coming next
roadmap.text: The near-term direction is to make EchoMind more dependable for daily planning and multilingual note workflows.
roadmap.li1: Cleaner recording organization and faster search.
roadmap.li2: More robust extraction of tasks, dates, and reminders.
roadmap.li3: Stronger sharing/export combinations for audio + text summaries.
roadmap.li4: Ongoing UI refinements driven by real usage feedback.

footer.note: EchoMind is in active development and this page will evolve together with the app.
